design_philosophy.core_loop: Select destinations on tactical map → engage enemies in real-time action perspective → return to map to manage resource allocation and Corneria's defense meter. Core tension between offensive action and defensive strategy.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Real-time threat management: player must balance pursuing enemy forces across the Lylat system against incoming missiles and fighter squadrons threatening planet Corneria. Leaving one battle to intercept another creates constant tactical pressure.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command a fighter team protecting your homeworld; pilot advanced Arwings while making strategic decisions about which threats to prioritize. Choice of pilot pairs and destination selection creates sense of tactical control.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Semi-real-time strategy hybrid that refuses to be purely linear or purely freeform. Game forces engagement with map-level strategy even during action sequences—time progresses on the map while player is in combat, making disengagement a strategic choice rather than a loading screen. This merges rail-shooter action with RTS decision-making in a way that defines its unique identity.

difficulty.lives_system: Two pilots with individual shield gauges. When one pilot's energy depletes, that Arwing explodes and pilot is permanently lost for the game session. Game continues with remaining pilot at reduced effectiveness.
difficulty.checkpointing: No traditional checkpoints. Failure condition: Corneria damage reaches 100% OR both pilots die. Progress resets to map screen after each action encounter or map traversal.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through pilot selection (6 characters with distinct capabilities) and destination prioritization. Player chooses which threats to engage based on map positioning. Satellite defense system requires active protection, adding optional strategic complexity. Difficulty emerges from time pressure and resource scarcity rather than artificial scaling.

progression.upgrade_system: Pilot rotation and swapping system serves as tactical upgrade mechanic. Choosing which two pilots to deploy affects capability profile (health, speed, firepower varies by character). Custom Arwings and Mothership mentioned as new equipment.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story progression reveals new enemy types, bases, mercenary encounters (Star Wolf team), and boss fights. Encounters appear to scale or branch based on which threats player addresses on the map, suggesting some encounter flexibility.
replayability_hooks: Six playable characters (Fox, Falco, Peppy, Slippy, Miyu, Fay) with different capabilities enabling varied team compositions
Semi-real-time map allows different approach orders to defend Corneria, creating variable strategic paths
Pilots can permanently fall in combat, creating high-stakes roguelike element that invites replay attempts with different team strategies
Boss encounters with Star Wolf mercenary team suggest multiple confrontations that may vary by player choices

world_building_techniques: Character relationships established through pilot selection (Fox's team: Falco as best friend, Peppy as mentor, Slippy as childhood friend; new recruits Miyu and Fay add fresh dynamics)
Lylat system geography serves as map-based world building—planets represent defended locations and strategic objectives
Enemy faction characterization: Star Wolf as rival mercenary pilots (Wolf O'Donnell as criminal leader, Pigma Dengar as traitor, Leon Powalski and Algy as specialized threats)
Antagonist continuation: Emperor Andross returns with escalated threat (fleet, missiles, bases), implying world-level consequences

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters emerge from story progression (Star Wolf team, Andross, planetary bases) and serve as climactic moments within larger defensive mission context. Bosses are integrated into the map-level strategy—player must choose when to confront them against other pressing threats.
notable_bosses: Star Wolf mercenary team (recurring rival pilots with individual specializations: Wolf O'Donnell, Leon Powalski, Pigma Dengar, Algy)
Planetary bases (fire IPBMs, deploy fighter squadrons)
Battleships (mobile threats that deploy additional fighter squadrons)

faction_indicators: Star Fox team (heroes, defensive alignment—Fox, Falco, Peppy, Slippy, Miyu, Fay)
Star Wolf mercenaries (antagonistic, morally compromised—Wolf O'Donnell, Leon Powalski, Pigma Dengar, Algy)
Emperor Andross faction (imperial aggressor, systematic threat through fleet and ballistic missiles)
danger_signals: Incoming IPBMs (interplanetary ballistic missiles) represent strategic time pressure—visible on map but require active interception
Corneria damage meter reaches 100% = mission failure, creating clear loss state telegraphed throughout gameplay
Pilot death is permanent within a single run, signaling high-stakes resource management
Battleships and bases deploy fighter squadrons, creating escalating threat complexity
Satellite takeover events (special enemies capture General Pepper's defense satellite) create vulnerability windows
environmental_storytelling: Lylat system map shows multiple contested planets as Andross's offensive targets, establishing scope of invasion
Planetary bases distributed across system imply deep network of enemy infrastructure
Corneria damage meter visualizes planet's degradation as battle progresses, making consequences visceral
